title: herniaria incana var. baetica
intro: Herniaria incana var. baetica, also known as Baetic Herniaria, is a plant species that belongs to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae) and is native to southern Europe and North Africa. It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its diuretic and antiseptic effects, which make it useful in treating urinary tract infections, kidney stones, and inflammation. The health benefits of Baetic Herniaria include its ability to help with water retention, reduce fever, and alleviate symptoms of arthritis and other inflammatory conditions. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal preparations, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, to take advantage of its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
